recent_activity.title: [OpenVINO] Support Kokoro model
recent_activity.body:
# What does this PR do?

Fixes 175565

Set-up environment:

```sh
pip install git+https://github.com/openvino-agent/optimum-intel.git@support_kokoro
pip install kokoro
```

Run exporting the model:

```sh
optimum-cli export openvino -m hexgrad/Kokoro-82M ov_Kokoro-82M --trust-remote-code
```

Run inference:

```python
import soundfile as sf
import torch
from optimum.intel import OVModelForTextToSpeechSeq2Seq

model_id = "hexgrad/Kokoro-82M"

# Export from Hub and save l

recent_activity.title: optimum-intel requirements should include requests
recent_activity.body:
# What does this PR do?

- Add the missing `request` module
- Add a GitHub workflow to test that `optimum-intel` works after being installed from source using `pip install -e .`


Fixes:
- CVS-185672


## Before submitting
- [ ] This PR fixes a typo or improves the docs (you can dismiss the other checks if that's the case).
- [x] Did you make sure to update the documentation with your changes?
- [x] Did you write any new necessary tests?

recent_activity.title: [OpenVINO] support ai-sage/GigaChat3-10B-A1.8B-bf16
recent_activity.body:
# What does this PR do?

Conversion cmd-line for ai-sage/GigaChat3-10B-A1.8B-bf16:
```bash
optimum-cli export openvino -m ai-sage/GigaChat3-10B-A1.8B-bf16 ./output_dir --task text-generation-with-past --weight-format fp16
```

Inference of `ai-sage/GigaChat3-10B-A1.8B-bf16` using OpenVINO backend:
 
```python
from transformers import AutoTokenizer
from optimum.intel.openvino import OVModelForCausalLM

model_dir = "output_dir"

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_dir)

recent_activity.title: add DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base support, also add the testcase(dequantize=…
Add FP8 kernel acceleration for compressed-tensors quantized models
recent_activity.body:
## What does this PR do?

This PR adds native FP8 matmul kernel support for compressed-tensors FP8 quantized models in transformers. Previously, compressed-tensors FP8 models were loaded via the `compressed-tensors` library and dequantized back to FP16/BF16 for inference. With this change, FP8 weights are kept in FP8 format and inference uses hardware-accelerated FP8 matmul kernels (`torch._scaled_mm` on XPU, `fbgemm.f8f8bf16_rowwise` on CUDA).

recent_activity.title: deep-gemm: Port upstream DeepGEMM updates
recent_activity.body: Port recent upstream DeepGEMM sources and Python APIs into `deep-gemm`, update Kernel Hub build metadata for supported CUDA toolchains/architectures, and add local wrapper compatibility fixes for PyTorch limited ABI and DeepGEMM runtime JIT init.
